notice: Curated recommendations only. These works are not supplied by this repository.

records.title: As I Lay Dying
records.author: William Faulkner
records.description: A poor Southern family transports its mother's body for burial.
records.why_included: Challenged for language, sexuality, abortion, death, religion, and family dysfunction.

records.title: Animal Farm
records.author: George Orwell
records.description: A revolution against human masters turns into a new hierarchy of power.
records.why_included: Essential fable of revolutionary ideals, controlled history, and oligarchy.

records.title: Brave New World
records.author: Aldous Huxley
records.description: A technocratic society uses conditioning, pleasure, and managed reproduction for control.
records.why_included: Essential counterpoint to coercive models of authoritarianism.

records.title: Fahrenheit 451
records.author: Ray Bradbury
records.description: A fireman begins preserving books in a culture organized around their destruction.
records.why_included: Defining modern novel of censorship and anti-intellectualism.

records.title: The Handmaid's Tale
records.author: Margaret Atwood
records.description: A woman survives under a theocratic regime that controls reproduction and literacy.
records.why_included: Essential fiction of religious authoritarianism and bodily autonomy.

records.title: The Dispossessed
records.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
records.description: A physicist moves between rival societies organized around scarcity, capitalism, and anarchism.
records.why_included: Nuanced exploration of freedom, bureaucracy, solidarity, and political systems.

records.title: Darkness at Noon
records.author: Arthur Koestler
records.description: An old revolutionary is forced through the logic of ideological confession and purge.
records.why_included: Essential novel of totalitarian confession and destruction of conscience.

records.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
records.author: Hannah Arendt
records.description: A study of antisemitism, imperialism, and totalitarian political organization.
records.why_included: Foundational framework for understanding totalitarian systems.

records.title: The Open Society and Its Enemies
records.author: Karl Popper
records.description: A philosophical defense of critical inquiry against closed political systems.
records.why_included: Major critique of historicism and authoritarian thought.

records.title: The Power of the Powerless
records.author: Václav Havel
records.description: An essay on ordinary conformity and dissent under ideological rule.
records.why_included: Practical and humane account of resistance within authoritarian systems.

records.title: The Gulag Archipelago
records.author: Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn
records.description: Documentary-literary history of Soviet forced labor and political repression.
records.why_included: Landmark testimony about state terror and institutional denial.

records.title: Seeing Like a State
records.author: James C. Scott
records.description: An account of how centralized schemes simplify people and places, often disastrously.
records.why_included: Essential critique of high-modernist administrative power.

records.title: On Tyranny
records.author: Timothy Snyder
records.description: Short historical lessons about defending institutions and civic habits.
records.why_included: Accessible starting point for recognizing democratic erosion.

records.title: Thought Reform and the Psychology of Totalism
records.author: Robert Jay Lifton
records.description: A model of ideological re-education and coercive social environments.
records.why_included: Foundational analysis of coercive persuasion.

records.title: Coercive Persuasion
records.author: Edgar H. Schein and colleagues
records.description: Academic study of forced ideological and behavioral change.
records.why_included: Important companion to practical propaganda and coercion material.

records.title: Influence
records.author: Robert Cialdini
records.description: Popular account of recurring mechanisms of compliance and persuasion.
records.why_included: Useful for recognizing manipulation in ordinary settings.

records.title: Manufacturing Consent
records.author: Edward S. Herman and Noam Chomsky
records.description: A structural model of media filtering and elite influence.
records.why_included: Important framework for evaluating information systems and ownership.

records.title: The True Believer
records.author: Eric Hoffer
records.description: A study of mass movements and ideological commitment.
records.why_included: Useful historical lens on identity, grievance, and collective belief.

records.title: Applied Cryptography
records.author: Bruce Schneier
records.description: Broad reference on cryptographic concepts and protocols.
records.why_included: Important historical and conceptual cryptography reference.

records.title: Cryptography Engineering
records.author: Niels Ferguson, Bruce Schneier, and Tadayoshi Kohno
records.description: Practical guidance on designing and evaluating cryptographic systems.
records.why_included: Strong bridge from historical ciphers to modern security concepts.

records.title: Permanent Record
records.author: Edward Snowden
records.description: Memoir of the modern surveillance state and the decisions behind public disclosure.
records.why_included: First-person account relevant to privacy and state surveillance.

records.title: Data and Goliath
records.author: Bruce Schneier
records.description: Examination of surveillance by governments and corporations.
records.why_included: Accessible framework for understanding data collection and power.

records.title: Weapons of Math Destruction
records.author: Cathy O'Neil
records.description: How opaque models can reinforce institutional inequality and error.
records.why_included: Important for understanding automated power and data literacy.

records.title: The Demon-Haunted World
records.author: Carl Sagan
records.description: A case for scientific skepticism and disciplined inquiry.
records.why_included: Essential companion to propaganda analysis and evidence literacy.

records.title: The Satanic Verses
records.author: Salman Rushdie
records.description: A novel of migration, faith, identity, and contested religious narrative.
records.why_included: One of the defining modern censorship controversies.

records.title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
records.author: George Orwell
records.description: A novel of surveillance, controlled language, manufactured reality, and political terror.
records.why_included: Essential fiction for understanding authoritarian information control.

records.title: The Rape of the Mind
records.author: Joost Meerloo
records.description: A study of coercive persuasion and totalitarian psychological control.
records.why_included: A foundational reference for recognizing coercive ideological environments.

records.title: Survival
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Shelter, water, fire, signaling, navigation, weather, and survival psychology.
records.why_included: Shelter, water, fire, signaling, navigation, weather, and survival psychology.

records.title: Map Reading and Land Navigation
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Maps, compass bearings, coordinates, terrain association, and route planning.
records.why_included: Maps, compass bearings, coordinates, terrain association, and route planning.

records.title: Rigging Techniques, Procedures and Applications
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Rope, knots, slings, hoists, anchors, and mechanical advantage.
records.why_included: Rope, knots, slings, hoists, anchors, and mechanical advantage.

records.title: First Aid
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Bleeding, shock, wounds, fractures, burns, and environmental injuries.
records.why_included: Bleeding, shock, wounds, fractures, burns, and environmental injuries.

records.title: Field Hygiene and Sanitation
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Waste disposal, camp hygiene, water, food sanitation, and disease prevention.
records.why_included: Waste disposal, camp hygiene, water, food sanitation, and disease prevention.

records.title: First Aid for Soldiers
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Compact historical first-aid reference.
records.why_included: Compact historical first-aid reference.

records.title: Map Reading
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Straightforward map-reading instruction.
records.why_included: Straightforward map-reading instruction.

records.title: Survival at Sea
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Flotation, exposure, water, signaling, and small-craft survival.
records.why_included: Flotation, exposure, water, signaling, and small-craft survival.

records.title: Care and Use of Individual Clothing and Equipment
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Clothing maintenance, load organization, weather protection, and field equipment.
records.why_included: Clothing maintenance, load organization, weather protection, and field equipment.

records.title: Evasion and Escape
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Concealment, movement, navigation, and improvised survival.
records.why_included: Concealment, movement, navigation, and improvised survival.

records.title: Engineer Field Data
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Construction tables, materials, measurements, and engineering data.
records.why_included: Construction tables, materials, measurements, and engineering data.

records.title: Concrete and Masonry
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Basic concrete, masonry, and structural repair.
records.why_included: Basic concrete, masonry, and structural repair.

records.title: Survival
records.author: United States Army
records.description: General field survival and individual fieldcraft.
records.why_included: General field survival and individual fieldcraft.

records.title: Chemical, Biological, and Nuclear Defense
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Historical recognition and protective principles.
records.why_included: Historical recognition and protective principles.

records.title: Ranger Handbook
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Navigation, knots, fieldcraft, communications, and planning.
records.why_included: Navigation, knots, fieldcraft, communications, and planning.

records.title: Water Support Operations
records.author: United States Army
records.description: Water logistics and support operations.
records.why_included: Water logistics and support operations.

records.title: Surveillance Self-Defense
records.author: Electronic Frontier Foundation
records.description: Current guidance on threat modeling, private communications, metadata, and device security.
records.why_included: A practical starting point for understanding privacy, safer communications, and surveillance-aware digital habits.

records.title: Free Culture
records.author: Lawrence Lessig
records.description: Argument about copyright, technology, creativity, and the commons.
records.why_included: Directly relevant to preserving and sharing culture lawfully.

records.title: Content
records.author: Cory Doctorow
records.description: Essays on digital rights, creativity, copyright, and technological control.
records.why_included: Strong companion to the archive's free-knowledge mission.

records.title: CryptoParty Handbook
records.author: CryptoParty contributors
records.description: Introductory computer and internet security guidance.
records.why_included: A practical introduction to privacy, encryption, and safer everyday computer use.

records.title: The Castle
records.author: Franz Kafka
records.description: A land surveyor struggles to obtain recognition from an inaccessible authority.
records.why_included: A major novel of bureaucracy, exclusion, and opaque power.

records.title: Amerika
records.author: Franz Kafka
records.description: An unfinished novel of migration, exploitation, absurd institutions, and modern alienation.
records.why_included: A major Kafka novel about migration, exploitation, absurd institutions, and modern alienation.

records.title: Berlin Alexanderplatz
records.author: Alfred Döblin
records.description: A working-class man tries to remake his life in Weimar Berlin.
records.why_included: Landmark city novel banned and burned by the Nazis.

records.title: The Magic Mountain
records.author: Thomas Mann
records.description: A visitor to a Swiss sanatorium encounters competing visions of Europe, illness, time, and politics.
records.why_included: One of the central modernist novels; Mann's works were banned and burned by the Nazis.

records.title: The Death of Ivan Ilyich
records.author: Leo Tolstoy
records.description: A successful official confronts illness, mortality, and the falseness of his social world.
records.why_included: Canonical novella about death, meaning, and social conformity.

records.title: Hadji Murad
records.author: Leo Tolstoy
records.description: A Caucasian resistance leader is caught between Russian imperial power and local rivalries.
records.why_included: Major anti-imperial and antiwar novella.

records.title: What Is to Be Done?
records.author: Nikolai Chernyshevsky
records.description: A politically influential novel of radical education, cooperative life, and social change.
records.why_included: Repeatedly suppressed for revolutionary ideas and clandestine political influence.

records.title: Kokoro
records.author: Natsume Sōseki
records.description: A novel of isolation, guilt, modernity, friendship, and suicide.
records.why_included: One of Japan's central modern novels.

records.title: I Am a Cat
records.author: Natsume Sōseki
records.description: A cat satirizes class, education, and intellectual pretension in modernizing Japan.
records.why_included: Canonical social satire with enduring cultural value.

records.title: Botchan
records.author: Natsume Sōseki
records.description: An impulsive teacher confronts hypocrisy and hierarchy at a provincial school.
records.why_included: Widely read Japanese classic about institutional authority.

records.title: Diary of a Madman
records.author: Lu Xun
records.description: A narrator sees oppressive tradition as a system of cannibalism.
records.why_included: Foundational modern Chinese story attacking conformist social authority.

records.title: The True Story of Ah Q
records.author: Lu Xun
records.description: A powerless rural man rationalizes humiliation amid social and political upheaval.
records.why_included: Foundational political and social satire in modern Chinese literature.

records.title: Rashōmon
records.author: Ryūnosuke Akutagawa
records.description: A servant confronts moral collapse and survival in a ruined Kyoto.
records.why_included: Canonical short story of social disorder and ethical ambiguity.

records.title: In a Grove
records.author: Ryūnosuke Akutagawa
records.description: Contradictory testimony makes the truth of a killing impossible to settle.
records.why_included: Influential study of uncertain truth and institutional testimony.

records.title: The Vortex
records.author: José Eustasio Rivera
records.description: Travelers enter a jungle economy built on rubber extraction, exploitation, and violence.
records.why_included: Major Latin American indictment of colonial and labor abuse.

records.title: Macunaíma
records.author: Mário de Andrade
records.description: A mythic antihero crosses a Brazil of folklore, language play, race, and modernity.
records.why_included: Foundational Brazilian modernist novel with challenging cultural and political themes.

records.title: Camp-Lore and Woodcraft
records.author: D. C. Beard
records.description: Fire, knots, camps, tools, and outdoor construction.
records.why_included: Broad practical source for fieldcraft and simple construction.

records.title: The Boy Mechanic, Volume 1
records.author: Popular Mechanics
records.description: Historical repair, tool, mechanism, and workshop projects.
records.why_included: Useful for improvisation and mechanical understanding; unsafe projects require modern review.

records.title: The Boy Mechanic, Volume 2
records.author: Popular Mechanics
records.description: Historical home workshop projects and repair ideas.
records.why_included: Companion volume for practical mechanics and improvisation.

records.title: The Boy Mechanic, Volume 3
records.author: Popular Mechanics
records.description: Historical projects in mechanics, construction, and tools.
records.why_included: Completes the early practical-mechanics set.

records.title: Handy Farm Devices and How to Make Them
records.author: Rolfe Cobleigh
records.description: Farm tools, storage devices, and small construction projects.
records.why_included: Practical resourcefulness and repair ideas for low-resource situations.

records.title: Henley's Twentieth Century Formulas, Recipes and Processes
records.author: Norman W. Henley
records.description: Historical materials, shop processes, and technical formulas.
records.why_included: Useful reference history, but contains hazardous and obsolete procedures.

records.title: The Preservation of Food in the Home
records.author: United States Department of Agriculture
records.description: Historical canning, drying, and food-storage guidance.
records.why_included: Useful historical context only; current USDA processing guidance takes precedence.

records.title: Manual of Gardening
records.author: L. H. Bailey
records.description: Home and market gardening methods.
records.why_included: Broad gardening reference; pesticide advice must be treated as obsolete.

records.title: Audel's Carpenters and Builders Guide
records.author: Theo. Audel and Company
records.description: Carpentry, construction, and building reference.
records.why_included: Strong practical reference when an eligible early edition is selected.

records.title: Food and Water in an Emergency
records.author: FEMA
records.description: Household food storage, emergency water, and power-loss preparation.
records.why_included: A concise federal reference for common disruptions.

records.title: Make Water Safe During an Emergency
records.author: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.description: Current guidance on boiling, disinfecting, and filtering emergency water.
records.why_included: High-value life-safety guidance that must remain current and dated.

records.title: Finding Your Way with Map and Compass
records.author: U.S. Geological Survey
records.description: Map scales, contours, bearings, and compass use.
records.why_included: Civilian navigation reference suitable for offline use.

records.title: Camping and Woodcraft
records.author: Horace Kephart
records.description: Campcraft, shelters, tools, fire, and wilderness living.
records.why_included: An enduring practical reference, with medical and conservation advice clearly marked as historical.

records.title: Emergency Financial First Aid Kit
records.author: FEMA and Ready.gov
records.description: Forms and guidance for documents, accounts, insurance, and disaster recovery.
records.why_included: Practical household continuity reference that is often overlooked.

records.title: Use Safe Water During an Emergency
records.author: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.description: Safe water use after contamination or disrupted service.
records.why_included: Current public-health companion to storage and treatment guidance.

records.title: Preventing Diarrheal Illness After a Disaster
records.author: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.description: Disaster sanitation, water, handwashing, and food-safety guidance.
records.why_included: Current health guidance for a high-risk post-disaster problem.

records.title: Household Emergency Plan and Supply Checklists
records.author: Ready.gov
records.description: Family communication, evacuation, and supply-planning forms.
records.why_included: Compact, actionable first-line preparedness material.

records.title: Wildfire Evacuation Materials
records.author: U.S. Fire Administration
records.description: Go-bags, evacuation timing, and wildfire preparation guidance.
records.why_included: Regionally critical planning material for fast-moving hazards.

records.title: Flood Safety and Cleanup Guides
records.author: FEMA and Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.description: Flood evacuation, contamination, cleanup, and mold guidance.
records.why_included: High-value, safety-critical disaster recovery reference.

records.title: Power Outage Food Safety Guidance
records.author: United States Department of Agriculture
records.description: Handling refrigerated and frozen food during electrical outages.
records.why_included: Current, actionable guidance that prevents avoidable illness.

records.title: Generator Safety Guidance
records.author: Consumer Product Safety Commission and Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
records.description: Carbon monoxide and safe generator-placement guidance.
records.why_included: Critical hazard-prevention material for common emergency equipment.

records.title: Radiological Emergency Guides
records.author: FEMA, CDC, and EPA
records.description: Official protective actions for radiological emergencies.
records.why_included: Preserve current official guidance rather than historical speculation.

records.title: Community Emergency Response Team Basic Training
records.author: FEMA CERT
records.description: Disaster preparedness, basic response, organization, and light rescue.
records.why_included: Structured civilian training material for neighborhood-scale resilience.

records.title: Rights of Man
records.author: Thomas Paine
records.description: A defense of popular sovereignty and a critique of hereditary government.
records.why_included: Provides a compact case for political rights independent of dynastic authority.

records.title: Civil Disobedience
records.author: Henry David Thoreau
records.description: An argument against cooperation with profoundly unjust government action.
records.why_included: Defends individual conscience against coercive state power.

records.title: Two Treatises of Government
records.author: John Locke
records.description: A defense of natural rights, consent of the governed, and limits on political authority.
records.why_included: Supplies an enduring argument for removing governments that violate public trust.

records.title: Capital, Volume I
records.author: Karl Marx
records.description: A critique of capital, labor, ownership, exploitation, and class domination.
records.why_included: Repeatedly banned or restricted under anticommunist and authoritarian governments.

records.title: The Age of Reason
records.author: Thomas Paine
records.description: A critique of revealed religion and scriptural authority.
records.why_included: Essential freethought text frequently condemned for its challenge to religious power.

records.title: Why I Am an Agnostic
records.author: Robert G. Ingersoll
records.description: An accessible defense of agnosticism and intellectual freedom.
records.why_included: Representative American freethought writing challenging clerical authority.

records.title: The Psychology of Suggestion
records.author: Boris Sidis
records.description: Early study of suggestibility, imitation, hypnosis, and mental influence.
records.why_included: Historical context for propaganda, persuasion, and coercive environments.

records.title: Index Librorum Prohibitorum
records.author: Catholic Church
records.description: Historical lists of publications prohibited by ecclesiastical authority.
records.why_included: Primary evidence of a long-running institutional censorship system.

records.title: Crime and Punishment
records.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
records.description: An impoverished student commits murder while testing whether he stands beyond ordinary morality.
records.why_included: A central world novel about ideology, poverty, conscience, policing, religion, and moral autonomy.

records.title: The Brothers Karamazov
records.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
records.description: A family drama of patricide, faith, doubt, freedom, guilt, and moral responsibility.
records.why_included: Commonly ranked among the greatest novels and repeatedly controversial for theology and political implications.

records.title: Notes from Underground
records.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
records.description: An isolated narrator attacks rational utopianism while exposing resentment and self-destruction.
records.why_included: A foundational existential work with political, religious, and anti-utopian significance.

records.title: Demons
records.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
records.description: Political radicals, manipulators, and idealists become entangled in conspiracy and murder.
records.why_included: Important for revolutionary politics, terrorism, atheism, religion, and extremism.

records.title: Dangerous Liaisons
records.author: Pierre Choderlos de Laclos
records.description: Aristocratic libertines manipulate lovers and rivals through seduction and correspondence.
records.why_included: Repeatedly restricted for sexual exploitation, adultery, and moral corruption.

records.title: Émile, or On Education
records.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
records.description: A philosophical account of education, human development, nature, society, and belief.
records.why_included: Burned and banned for its religious arguments and challenge to established authority.

records.title: We
records.author: Yevgeny Zamyatin
records.description: A mathematician in a totalitarian glass city develops imagination, desire, and rebellion.
records.why_included: Banned in the Soviet Union and foundational to dystopian fiction.
